chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 413 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge 1. For order on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of bail application 27.11.2023 Ms. Babra Solangi, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy Prosecutor General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J. – Applicant is a brother of complainant. There is a dispute between them over a property matter. Applicant along with two other brothers and co-accused Naeem Suhag and Ali Muhammad is alleged to attack complainant on 14.01.2023, at a link road in Deh Kingri within jurisdiction of Police Station Pir-Jo-Goth, causing him multiple injuries. Main injury with a hatchet on his head is attributed to co-accused Naeem Suhag whose bail application has been rejected up to this Court. However, applicant and his two brothers are alleged to have caused injuries on different parts of his body, which injuries, as per medical record, although are non-bailable but are minor in nature and punishable up to only five years. Therefore, to the extent of applicant, applicability of Section 324 PPC requires further enquiry. More so, applicant is in jail since 20.01.2023, and so far no progress has been made in the trial. Therefore, learned DPG has conceded. 2. Accordingly, this application is allowed, and applicant is granted post-arrest bail subject to furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/- (Rupees one lac) and P.R. bond of the same amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 3. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial Court while deciding the case on merits.

Accordingly, this application is allowed, and applicant is granted post-arrest bail subject to furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/- (Rupees one lac) and P.R. bond of the same amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 3. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial Court while deciding the case on merits. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
